zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret le Boteler於1249年出生在英國瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林時，她的父親William le Boteler21歲，母親Dionysia Lostock15歲。她於1267年與Richard de Radcliffe在英國英格蘭藍卡爾沃林頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1339年在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特享年90歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret le Boteler于1249年出生在英国瓦維其可爾奧維斯利 格林时，她的父亲William le Boteler21岁，母亲Dionysia Lostock15岁。她于1267年与Richard de Radcliffe在英国英格兰藍卡爾沃林頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1339年在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特享年90岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret le Boteler was born in 1249, in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, her father, William le Boteler, was 21 and her mother, Dionysia Lostock, was 15. She married Richard de Radcliffe in 1267, in Warrington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1339, in Manchester, Lancashire, England, at the age of 90, and was buried in Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret le Boteler est née en 1249, à Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, William le Boteler, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Dionysia Lostock, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Richard de Radcliffe en 1267, à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1339, à Manchester, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterrée à Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret le Boteler 1249 in Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William le Boteler 21 und ihre Mutter Dionysia Lostock 15. Sie hat 1267 in Warrington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Richard de Radcliffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1339 im Alter von 90 Jahren in Manchester, Lancashire, England und wurde in Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret le Boteler è nata il nel 1249 nel Oversley Green, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, William le Boteler, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Dionysia Lostock, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Richard de Radcliffe nel 1267, nel Warrington, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1339, nel Manchester, Lancashire, England, all'età di 90 anni, e fu sepolta a Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret le Botelerが1249年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアオバースリー グレンで生まれた時，父William le Botelerは21歳，母Dionysia Lostockは15歳であった。1267年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンでRichard de Radcliffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1339年にイギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスターで90歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret le Boteler(이)가 1249년 영국 워윅실르 버슬리 그린에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William le Boteler(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dionysia Lostock(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1267년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 워링턴에서 Richard de Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1339년 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르에 묻혔다.

mn.Bewsey Hall, Warrington, Lancashire, Eng: Warrington, Lancashire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret le Boteler nasceu em 1249, em Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William le Boteler, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Dionysia Lostock, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard de Radcliffe em 1267, em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1339, em Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 90 anos, e foi sepultada em Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret le Boteler родилась в 1249 г. в местности овирсли грин, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William le Boteler было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Dionysia Lostock, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Radcliffe в 1267 г. в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1339 г. в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret le Boteler nació en 1249, en Oversley Green, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William le Boteler, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Dionysia Lostock, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Richard de Radcliffe en 1267, en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1339, en Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 90 años, y fue sepultada en Lancashire, Inglaterra.
